text: Here we investigated physicochemical and antioxidant features of 10 honeys produced by *Apis mellifera* L. from apiaries in Roraima state, Brazil. Total phenolic content, flavonoid composition and honey color intensity and the ability of honey extracts to scavenge free radicals were determined. Total phenolic content assessed using Folin-Ciocalteu method varied from 250 to 548 mg gallic acid equivalent per kilogram of honey. Flavonoids were quantified as total flavones/flavonols using aluminium chloride method (9–48.6 mg quercetin per kilogram of honey) and flavanones/dihydroflavonols using 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine method (1805–2606 mg pinocembran per kilogram of honey). The ability of honey extracts to scavengege the free radicals assessed by using the DPPH assay showed that inhibitory concentrations at 50 per cent (IC₅₀) values for honey extracts varied from 3.17 to 8.79 mg/mL. High degree of correlations between honey color intensity, total flavones/flavonols content, total phenolic content and DPPH IC₅₀ values were confirmed by factor analysis indicating that stronger antioxidant activities in darker honey samples with higher polyphenolic content.
explanation: The text reads as a plausible, domain-accurate abstract with realistic experimental details; minor typos (scavengege, pinocembran) suggest human authorship, making AI generation less likely.
